title: Glorious Light Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A light and flavorful Malaysian dish featuring chicken satay with a mustardy and slightly sweet taste, perfect for showing off at any dinner party. This recipe is nut-free and features a shio-koji cure for added depth of flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together shio-koji, brown sugar, grated ginger, red pepper flakes, and mustard powder to create the cure.
Add the chicken strips to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the cure.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken strips onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill or grill pan with vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the chicken satay for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, brush the red bell pepper strips with vegetable oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the red bell pepper strips for 2-3 minutes per side, or until tender.
Serve the chicken satay with grilled red bell pepper strips and a side of coconut milk for dipping.
